channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Texas DOGE bill passes Senate to streamline state regulations
item.description: Texas SB14, a DOGE-like bill, has passed the Texas Senate and now will be heading to the House for a Vote. The bill, if passed, will streamline state regulations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: States move to ban or restrict use of synthetic dyes used in candies and cereal
item.description: Synthetic dyes used to make brightly colored cereals, drinks and candies are coming under scrutiny in states across the country.
item.dc:creator: Leah Willingham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court won't lift order that barred Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law. A split three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US robotics companies push for national strategy, including a central office, to compete with China
item.description: American robotics companies are pushing for a national robotics strategy, including a central government robotics office, to promote the industry at a time that Beijing is making intelligent robots a top national priority.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A bill proposing a near-total abortion ban causes an uproar at Georgia's Capitol
item.description: Protesters on each side of the abortion debate have packed a hallway of Georgia's Capitol during a hearing on a bill that would ban most abortions.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements for buying the kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Appeals court blocks Trump admin's deportation flights in Alien Enemies Act immigration suit
item.description: A federal appeals court issued a decision in a high-profile immigration case challenging the Trump administration's authority to deport Venezuelan nationals via a 1798 wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Hoax Signal
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Facing possible primary challenge from Trump ally, long-serving senator gets backing of leader Thune
item.description: Republican Sen. John Cornyn of Texas, facing a possible 2026 primary challenge from Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, a President Trump ally, lands the endorsement of Senate Majority Leader John Thune.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US could run short of money to pay its bills by August without a debt limit deal, CBO says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is on track to hit its statutory debt ceiling — the so-called X-date when the country runs short of money to pay its bills— as early as August without a deal between lawmakers and the White House, according to a Congressional Budget Office report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge struggles with scope of relief for fired workers
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge in Maryland said Wednesday he will at least briefly extend a temporary order requiring the Trump administration to bring back federal workers who were fired as part of a dramatic downsizing of the federal workforce, but the judge said he was struggling with the scope
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries calls for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to resign
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries tells NewsNation that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth should lose his job.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard calls Signal chats a 'mistake' as Trump officials face grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Wednesday it was a “mistake” for national security officials to discuss sensitive military plans on a group text chain that also included a journalist — a leak that has roiled President Donald Trump's national security leadership
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration is at the Supreme Court with a new emergency appeal, this time seeking approval to go ahead with cuts of hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Trump’s new executive order could upend voting
item.description: The order tests the power of Trump’s authority and would require voters using a federal form to show proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections. It’s sure to be tested in court.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: NYC Council to vote on new rules for scaffolding, sidewalk sheds
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council is set to take up a series of bills aimed at cracking down on scaffolding and sidewalk sheds.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits the El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has visited the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are members of the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang have been held since their removal from the U_S_ Noem’s trip Wednesday to the prison where inmates are pac
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida 'inmate bill of rights' would require improving prison conditions
item.description: A Tampa Bay lawmaker is fighting for legislation that would protect the rights of prisoners in the state's corrections system.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: DHS suspends green card processing for refugees, asylees
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said it is pausing review of green card applications for some refugees and asylees, leaving in limbo those who came to the U.S. after fleeing unrest. The directive from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Youngkin vetoes AI regulation legislation in Virginia
item.description: Youngkin said his administration and the state of Virginia already has established safeguards and oversight for AI use.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Atlantic editor suggests he's open to sharing Hegseth's full war plans texts publicly
item.description: Goldberg reported he saw in a message, sent to the group by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, specific weapons systems, human targets and other top secret information.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: The inside story of how a journalist was sent White House war plans
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, the editor of The Atlantic, said he was mistakenly added to a group chat with U.S. national security leaders about imminent military strikes on Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan, Ailsa Chang, Christopher Intagliata, and Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Judge contends Nazis got more due process than Trump deportees did
item.description: The fight over the rarely used wartime power has become central to Trump’s immigration crackdown agenda and his efforts to stretch the powers of the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Nassau Democrats block County Executive Blakeman's $400M plan
item.description: LONG ISLAND, N.Y. (PIX11) – Nassau Democrats have accused County Executive Bruce Blakeman of withholding critical first responder funding for their districts – and have refused to approve his $400 million plan for the county.
item.dc:creator: Kirstin Cole, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump officials' war planning group chat breach
item.description: Washington was rocked Monday by a truly extraordinary story. The editor in chief of The Atlantic, Jeffrey Goldberg, recounted how he had — apparently inadvertently — been added to a group chat featuring some of the most senior members of the United States government.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: 2 months into Trump's second administration, the news industry faces challenges from all directions
item.description: During the first Trump administration, many journalists worried most about being called “fake news.” They now face a more assertive President Donald Trump in his second term as a blitz of action by his new administration has journalists on their heels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Sanders at North Las Vegas rally says GOP, Dems out of touch with working families
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Vermont Independent Sen. Bernie Sanders and Democratic Rep. New York Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez held a rally Thursday in North Las Vegas, calling for a new way to defeat President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News
